lever: de-delegate: run networkx's exact Knuth/Szwarcfiter algorithm in-process on a one-time plain-Python adjacency snapshot (list(G) + list(G.successors(u))), skipping the fnx->nx full-graph conversion. Algorithm only touches in-degree (D init = node order) + out_edges (successor adjacency order), so the order-sensitive ordering stream is byte-identical.

parity.edge_cases: empty -> [()], single -> [(7,)], cyclic -> raises NetworkXUnfeasible lazily

headline: all_topological_sorts simple DiGraph: ~4-11x slower than nx -> FASTER than nx, 5.1-13x self-speedup (grows with n), byte-exact. Conversion tax was the entire overhead. Pure Python, no rebuild. MultiDiGraph keeps nx delegation (per-parallel-edge counts).
scope_note: antichains is at PARITY (nx compute dominates conversion, not a gap). dominating_set TBD.
